description: 'naweso' is a collective decision condition defined by the procedure using it. It reports that the stated threshold for unresolved objection has been met among the people counted as participants. 'nawo' (agree) is one person's stance, while consensus belongs to the group process and may coexist with reservations.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the sound through the nose, and open into central 'a'. Round the lips for voiced 'w', then move forward and unround into stressed true-mid 'e'. Form a narrow groove along the tongue for unvoiced 's' and finish on true-mid rounded 'o'.
sound_symbolism: Dental 'na' recalls the opening of 'nawo' (agree). Stressed 'we' widens through a lip glide before final 'so' gathers the air into a rounded hiss. Several distinct contacts settle into one contour.
usage_notes: One procedural finding can be counted; broad discussion treats consensus as a mass noun. The participants and blocking conditions give a particular consensus its meaning, with any reservations recorded beside it. Unanimity, consent, harmony, and legitimacy remain different claims.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Consensus can support cooperative self-governance when the participants and blocking conditions remain visible.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A procedural noun prevents silence or fatigue from being mistaken automatically for shared agreement.

semantic_domains.communication: participants' stated positions considered under a consensus procedure
semantic_domains.community: a collective decision condition defined for a particular group
